(07:47 GMT) Centrica Posts Lower Profit, Revenue

By Nina Kienle

Centrica backed its 2025 guidance despite a decline in last year's profit and revenue.

The U.K. energy company, which owns British Gas, said Thursday that for the full 12 months ended Dec. 31 pretax profit fell to 1.68 billion pounds ($2.11 billion)from 6.47 billion pounds from the prior year.

Adjusted pretax profit fell to 1.60 billion pounds from 2.71 billion pounds.

Net profit fell to 1.33 billion pounds from 3.93 billion pounds. The figure beat analysts' forecast of 972 million pounds, according to consensus estimates provided by Visible Alpha.

Revenue fell to 19.91 billion pounds from 26.46 billion pounds.

The utility said it is proposing a final dividend per share increase of 13% to 4.5 pence.

The company said it extended its share buyback to an additional 500 million pounds, taking the total program to 2.0 billion pounds, to be completed around the end of 2025.

For 2025, the company backed its outlook, consistent with its December business statement. The group aims for a 1.6 billion pounds run-rate of Ebitda by the end of 2028 and intends to increase 2025 dividend per share to 5.5 pence, it said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: Centrica's FY24 Profit, Total Group Revenue Falls
data.description:
Centrica said Thursday its profit and total group revenue for the full year 2024 fell year over year.

Profit attributable to owners of the parent for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 1.33 billion pounds sterling, compared with 3.93 billion pounds a year ago. EPS moved to 0.251 pound from 0.694 pound.

The integrated energy company's total group revenue was 19.91 billion pounds, compared with 26.46 billion pounds earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet were expecting 24.2 billion pounds in sales, 988.9 million pounds in net income, and 0.19 pound in EPS.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a final dividend of 0.03 pound per share for the period, payable on June 5 to shareholders on record May 2. It paid 0.0267 pound per share a year ago. The final dividend takes the full-year 2024 dividend to 0.045 pound per share, up from 0.04 pound per share in 2023.

Looking ahead, the company intends to raise the full-year dividend in 2025 to 0.055 pound per share.

data.title: Danish Consumer Morale Lowest Since 2023
data.description:
The consumer confidence index in Denmark declined to -14.5 in February 2025 from -11.7 in the previous month.

This was the lowest reading since May 2023, as consumers' expectations regarding their finances became more pessimistic (-4.8 vs -1 in January), along with views on the economic situation in the coming year (-29.3 vs -18.6).

At the same time, the climate for major purchases was also less negative (-12.6 vs -17.8).

Meanwhile, assessments of families' financial situations compared to a year ago slightly improved (-5.2 vs -5.3).

Regarding aspects not factored into the confidence indicator, expectations for price development in the coming year strengthened (37 vs 29.2), while expectations for unemployment trends over the next twelve months heightened (16.2 vs. 10.1).

data.title: Petards Group's Subsidiary Gets Contract Valued at More Than $500,000
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Petards Group's subsidiary QRO Solutions won a contract valued at more than 400,000 pounds ($503,480) for in-vehicle automatic number plate recognition systems, one of the largest orders ever secured by the company.

The London-Listed developer of advanced security and surveillance systems said Thursday that the order was placed by an existing U.K. Police Force customer and is for the supply of its subsidiary's ANPR systems.

Initial deliveries of the systems will start during the first quarter of this year and all deliveries are expected to be completed during 2025.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Naturgy Board to Propose Tender Offer to Acquire up to 10% of Treasury Stock
BofA Expects 'Cautious' Rate Cutting Cycle in UK After January's Upside Inflation Surprise
data.description:
BofA Global Research on Wednesday upwardly revised its near-term UK inflation forecasts, noting its view that a rate cut from the Bank of England is "unlikely" in March.

The research firm's headline inflation estimate was raised to 3.1% from 2.7% for 2025 and to 2.2% from 2.1% for 2026, citing the hotter-than-expected January print and the expected increase in water bills in April. The UK's annual inflation accelerated to 3% from 2.5%, surpassing the consensus and BoE forecast of 2.8%.

Analysts added that the latest inflation reading supports a "cautious" cutting cycle with risks for fewer rate cuts than more.

"While we revise our inflation forecasts higher, a majority of the upward revision reflects one-off factors like the rise in energy and water bills," BofA said. "We expect domestic inflation to moderate slowly after April. We therefore don't expect this to derail our view of three quarterly cuts in 2025 with the next cut in May."

data.title: Naturgy to Offer EUR26.50 a Share
Hays Net Fees Fall Amid Hiring Slowdown
data.description:
By Maitane Sardon

U.K. recruiter Hays said net fees fell during its fiscal first half as it continued to grapple with a slowdown in hiring activity.

Hays reported pretax profit for the six months ended Dec. 31 of 19 million pounds ($23.9 million) compared with 55.5 million for the same period a year earlier.

Net fees--a closely watched industry metric--fell to 496 million pounds from 583.3 million pounds, a 13% decrease on a like-for-like basis. Within this, temporary fees were down 9% and permanent fees fell 19%.

Pre-exceptional operational profit came to 25.5 million pounds, in line with what the company had already reported in its January performance update. The figure came at the lower range of a company-compiled consensus of between 24.0 million and 33.2 million pounds and below the 60.1 million pounds it reported for the first half of 2023.

Temporary and contracting return to work volumes are building in line with the prior year in the U.K. and Ireland and in Australia and New Zealand, although are modestly behind in Germany, the company said. Permanent hiring remains tough overall, with slower decision-making impacting time to hire, it added.

The company declared a dividend of 0.95 pence a share.

"Temporary and contracting net fees are growing strongly in several of our focus countries. However, we continue to face considerable headwinds from economic conditions," Chief Executive Dirk Hahn said.

Hays' results come as recruiters grapple with negative economic sentiment in the U.K. The government's plan to raise payroll taxes from April, announced in October's budget, has led to increased business uncertainty, with some employers announcing plans to raise prices and cut headcount to deal with rising employment costs.

Write to Maitane Sardon at maitane.sardon@wsj.com

data.title: Anglo American Posts $3 Billion Loss After Large De Beers Impairment
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Anglo American posted a worse loss than expected as the miner booked $3.8 billion in net impairments, including $2.9 billion at its De Beers diamond business.

The London-listed company reported a net loss of $3.1 billion for 2024 compared with the $116.9 million loss analysts had expected, according to a Visible Alpha compiled consensus.

The diversified miner reported underlying earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization--its preferred profit metric--of $8.46 billion compared with the $8.76 billion analysts had expected and the $9.96 billion it booked in 2022.

Anglo said its work to separate De Beers is well under way but wrote down the value of the diamond business by $2.9 billion, citing prevailing marking conditions.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Herald Investment Trust Logs Higher FY24 Profit; Income Falls
data.description:
Herald Investment Trust said Thursday that profit for full-year 2024 rose year over year, while income declined.

Profit after tax for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 134.3 million pounds sterling, compared with 48.1 million pounds earlier. EPS moved to 2.4973 pounds from 0.8114 pound.

The investment trust's income totaled 17.2 million pounds, compared with 17.9 million pounds a year ago.

data.title: Ithaca Energy's FY24 Production Meets Top End of Guidance
data.description:
Ithaca Energy said Thursday that its full-year 2024 production met the top end of the market guidance range.

The UK North Sea-focused exploration and production company logged production of 80,200 barrels of oil equivalent per day for the year, compared with its guidance range of 76,000 and 81,000 barrels of oil equivalent per day.

Ithaca Energy reaffirmed a total dividend target of $500 million for 2024 and 2025.

Buy KEI Industries; target of Rs 4103: Geojit Financial Services
data.description:
Geojit Financial Services research report on KEI Industries

KEI Industries Ltd (KEI) is a leading manufacturer of cables and wires. It was established as a partnership firm in 1968 and incorporated as a public limited company in 1992. Consolidated revenue from operations increased 19.8% YoY to Rs. 2,467cr, driven by strong demand in the Cables and Wires (C&W) segment, particularly for infrastructure and industrial projects. Revenue from the C&W segment grew 26.0% YoY to Rs. 2,352cr, boosted by a substantial increase in domestic institutional cable sales and a notable 31% YoY increase in export sales. Revenue from EPC projects plunged 79.9% YoY to Rs. 76cr, in line with the company’s strategic decision to scale back its focus on EPC projects and prioritise its C&W segment to capitalise on the robust demand. At the operating level, EBITDA was up 11.3% YoY to Rs. 254cr in Q3FY25 and the company targets to eventually reach 11-12.5% in 2-3 years. However, the EBITDA margin declined to 10.3% from 11.1% in the last year, primarily owing to fluctuations in raw material costs, thereby increasing the cost of sales. PAT was at Rs. 165cr in Q3FY25, up 9.4% YoY, driven by robust top-line growth.

Outlook

The company’s strategic focus on meeting this heightened demand is evident from its ongoing capacity expansion, which positions it to capitalise on future opportunities and improve operational efficiencies. Hence, we upgrade our rating on the stock to BUY based on 36x FY27E P/E with a rolled-forward target price of Rs. 4,103.

data.title: South Korean Shares Decline for First Time in Seven Days on Lackluster Domestic Econ Data
data.description:
South Korean shares closed lower on Thursday, breaking a seven-day winning streak, after the country reported higher producer prices in January.

The Korea Composite Stock Price Index, or Kospi, was down by 17.46 points, or 0.7%, to close at 2,654.06. The Kosdaq fell 10 points, or 1.3%, to 768.27.

In economic news, Producer prices in South Korea increased 1.7% in January from a year earlier, maintaining the pace of growth in the previous month, according to preliminary data from the Bank of Korea on Thursday.

The on-year gains were primarily driven by increases across prices for agricultural foods, livestock products, and marine foods, manufacturing products, and services, which rose 4%, 0.6%, and 0.4%, respectively, the preliminary data indicated.

The prices for electric power, gas, water and waste remained unchanged in the month.

On a month-on-month basis, producer prices rose 0.6% in January.

In other economic news, South Korea's composite consumer sentiment index increased by 4 points to 95.2 in February from 91.2 in January, according to data released by the Bank of Korea on Thursday.

A reading above 100 indicates an improvement in outlook, while a reading under 100 suggests pessimism.

In corporate news, Hanwha Aerospace and Estonia's Milrem Robotics signed a memorandum of understanding to jointly develop and market robotic combat vehicles (RCV) and other ground robotic systems.

The partnership aims to enhance tracked RCV capabilities to meet customer needs and support mechanized units.

The firms will also execute global marketing efforts to expand the adoption of their advanced robotic solutions.

Shares of Hanwha Aerospace rose nearly 1% at market close on Thursday.

In other news, Lotte Chemical plans to divest its affiliate in Pakistan, Lotte Chemical Pakistan as part of its cost-cutting measures, according to a bourse filing.

The company sold its 75.01% stake in Lotte Chemical Pakistan for 97.9 billion won.

Lotte Chemical Pakistan was sold to a consortium that comprised AsiaPak Investments and Montage Oil DMCC.

Shares of Lotte Chemical rose more than 2% at market close.

Lloyds Banking Group Books Extra Provision For Motor Finance Probe, Denting Profit
data.description:
By Elena Vardon

Lloyds Banking Group took a further provision in the fourth quarter for the potential hit of a possible redress linked to its motor finance business, leading its pretax profit for the period to miss expectations.

The British lender on Thursday said it set 700 million pounds ($881.1 million) aside to cover the potential impact of historical commission arrangements on car loans. It had already booked a 450 million pound provision at its third-quarter results.

The group has been grappling with the uncertainty in the motor-finance sector amid a landmark case and regulatory review. Lloyds is the largest provider of motor finance in the U.K. and is exposed to these issues through its Black Horse brand.

For the three months ended Dec. 31, Lloyds posted pretax profit of 824 million pounds compared with 1.775 billion pounds for the same period a year earlier and a 1.25 billion pound estimate taken from a company-compiled consensus.

The bank--which is the country's largest mortgage provider--reported an on-year 1% rise in net income to 4.38 billion pounds, slightly beating consensus' 4.34 billion pound estimate. Net interest income made up 3.28 billion pounds of the total, against expectations of 3.25 billion pounds.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

(07:37 GMT) Repsol Swings to Quarterly Loss Amid Production, Energy Price Declines

By Pierre Bertrand

Repsol reported a decline in earnings, swinging to a quarterly loss, after production and energy prices fell in the final quarter of last year.

The Spanish energy company swung to a 36 million euro ($37.5 million) net loss for the fourth quarter, compared with 383 million in profit a year prior, on revenue which fell 9.4% to 14.05 billion euros.

On an adjusted basis, profit fell 46% to 643 million euros, but exceeded analysts' expectations of 600 million euros, according to a company-provided consensus.

The company said Thursday that its result reflected a 6.9% decline in hydrocarbon production, and a fall in international crude oil and natural gas prices during the quarter.

It added that it would propose a final dividend of 0.5 euros a share for 2024, adding that shareholders would receive a remuneration of 0.975 euros this year.

The company said it intends to launch a minimum share buyback of 700 million euros.

It has guided for production of 530,000 to 550,000 barrels of oil equivalent a day for this year, and said it expects to achieve between 6 billion and 6.5 billion euros in cash from operations.

Repsol expects to have 3.5 billion to 4 billion euros in capital expenditures this year.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: Correct: ChiNext Price Index Ends 0.1% Lower at 2225.58, Not Ends Flat
Pulsar International Files Cyber Complaint Against YouTube Channel for Spreading Misleading Information
data.description:
Pulsar International said it has filed a cyber police complaint against the YouTube channel "Nivesh Darshan" for spreading false and misleading information regarding the company's stock potential.

The company clarified that the video contains inaccurate details and was uploaded with the apparent intent to mislead investors for personal gain.

data.title: Mondi Pretax Profit Falls on Soft Demand, Pricing Challenges
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

Mondi's pretax profit fell last year due to soft demand combined with a challenging pricing environment.

The London-listed paper-and-packaging company said Thursday that pretax profit fell 45% on year to 378 million euros, equivalent to $394 million.

Underlying earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization--a company preferred metric that strips out exceptional and other one-off items--declined to 1.05 billion euros from 1.20 billion euros, with the margin dropping to 14.1% from 16.4%. This reflects a significantly lower forestry fair value gain and a 32 million one-off currency loss recognized in the first half from the devaluation of the Egyptian pound.

Revenue rose to 7.42 billion euros from 7.33 billion euros, missing market consensus of 7.50 billion euros from Visible Alpha.

The board declared a dividend of 70 European cents a share, flat on year.

"As we move into 2025, while significant macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainties remain, we are currently seeing improving order books across our packaging businesses and are implementing price increases across our range of packaging paper grades," Chief Executive Officer Andrew King said.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Jilin OLED Material Tech Unit Wins Bid for 655-Million-Yuan Project
data.description:
Jilin OLED Material Tech subsidiary Shanghai Shengxi won the bid for Chengdu BOE Display Technology's 8.6th-generation AMOLED production line project.

The 655.4-million-yuan contract involves the supply of a linear evaporation source system, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the advanced materials manufacturer closed less than 1% higher Thursday.

data.title: Airbus's Guidance Could Weigh on Shares — Market Talk
data.description: Airbus's weaker-than-expected guidance for the year could weigh on the European plane maker's shares Thursday, JPMorgan analysts write in a research note. Airbus expects to deliver about 820 commercial aircraft to customers this year, slightly below a JPMorgan forecast of 824 deliveries. The company also expects adjusted EBIT of around 7 billion euros, below a JPMorgan forecast of 7.48 billion euros. However, the analysts say that shares could receive some support if company executives were to suggest that guidance is conservative during their earnings call. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)
